input.command:
python3 -c "
import openpyxl
wb = openpyxl.load_workbook('input.xlsx')
print('Sheet names:', wb.sheetnames)
ws = wb['Data']
print(f'Dimensions: {ws.min_row}-{ws.max_row} rows, {ws.min_column}-{ws.max_column} cols')
# Print header
headers = [ws.cell(1, c).value for c in range(1, ws.max_column+1)]
print('Headers:', headers)
# Print column I values
print('Column I values:')
for r in range(1, ws.max_row+1):
    val = ws.cell(r, 9).value
    print(f'  Row {r}: {repr(val)}')
"
input.description: Read input.xlsx structure and column I values

input.command:
python3 -c "
import openpyxl

wb = openpyxl.load_workbook('input.xlsx')
ws = wb['Data']

# Filter column I for 'HUB Notes' and delete those rows (excluding header row 1)
# Work backwards to avoid row index shifting
rows_to_delete = []
for r in range(2, ws.max_row + 1):
    if ws.cell(r, 9).value == 'HUB Notes':
        rows_to_delete.append(r)

# Delete in reverse order
for r in reversed(rows_to_delete):
    ws.delete_rows(r)

print(f'Deleted {len(rows_to_delete)} rows')
print(f'Remaining rows: {ws.max_row}')
for r in range(1, ws.max_row + 1):
    print(f'  Row {r}: col I = {repr(ws.cell(r, 9).value)}')

wb.save('output.xlsx')
print('Saved output.xlsx')
"
input.description: Filter and delete HUB Notes rows, save output
